mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase discount rates applied to equity cash flows, reducing present valuations and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in high-rate periods are not significantly lower than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5%, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and higher recession probability, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real personal income growth outpacing inflation indicates expanding real purchasing power, which drives corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample regression, or 60-day forward returns show no directional bias when income outpaces inflation.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, a positive month-over-month change in industrial production signals rapid capacity utilization and earnings revisions, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Short-term returns fail to outperform baseline during recovery phases, or Rank IC drops below 0.01 across multiple post-shock periods.

mechanism_narrative: Higher housing starts reflect strong consumer confidence and construction sector activity, which correlates with positive S&P 500 forward returns as economic momentum builds.
failure_condition: Housing starts lose predictive power with Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling windows, or forward returns show no positive correlation with HOUST levels.

mechanism_narrative: When core inflation exceeds 315, markets anticipate aggressive central bank tightening and margin compression, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Inflation threshold fails to predict negative returns, with SPX posting positive 20d returns in > 55% of triggered cases over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate indicates a robust labor market expansion, supporting consumer spending and driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint labor signal shows no incremental predictive value over single proxies, or 60-day forward returns are randomly distributed across triggered states.

mechanism_narrative: During a late-cycle expansion regime, a quarterly decline in real GDP signals economic deceleration and earnings downgrade risk, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: GDP decline fails to predict negative returns during late-cycle phases, or Rank IC remains below 0.03 across all tested late-cycle windows.
